scenarios.title: Session 1 — a finding is born
scenarios.description: A researcher asks a survival-stratification question on TCGA LUAD. The agent runs it for real, reports the effect, and writes it to the graph with provenance and a calibrated novelty note.
preconditions: The seed graph is loaded (the abandoned finding present, the live finding absent)
The MCP server is connected to Claude Desktop
steps: Ask: in lung adenocarcinoma, is high cytotoxic T-cell infiltration associated with better overall survival?
The agent runs the analysis and reports HR, CI, p, and n
The agent writes the finding to the graph
expected: A real effect size is reported, not a placeholder
The novelty note is calibrated: no prior internal work found; the published record shows related work
The finding is written with author, date, data source, and method

scenarios.title: Session 2 — the save
scenarios.description: The whole pitch in one exchange. A different researcher, a new session, nothing in context. Rather than cold-starting, the agent tells them what their own org already knows — including the dead end a teammate already hit.
preconditions: Session 1 has run and its finding is in the graph
A new chat session with no shared context
steps: Ask a related question — the same signature in a different indication (LUSC)
The agent calls check_duplication and recall_findings before running anything
expected: The agent surfaces the Session 1 finding, naming who found it and what they found
The agent surfaces the abandoned attempt and the reason it was abandoned
The agent adds published context and names what is genuinely still open
Nothing is claimed to be novel

scenarios.title: The graph outlives the demo
scenarios.description: The closing beat: the durable artifact. A wiki page and a graph visual that nobody wrote, derived entirely from what the team actually did — including what failed.
steps: Render the wiki from the graph
expected: The wiki cites both demo findings and is marked generated and read-only
The visual shows both findings and the edge between them, with no hand-drawn nodes
